sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! The green bar is back to 14 pixels (full), and the yellow bar is at 14 pixels (shrunk). So clicking on the red dot inside the green bar (at (10, 28) when shrunk) caused the green bar to grow back to full!
This is a key discovery! Clicking on the red dot inside a bar causes the bar to GROW (or restore to full).
So the mechanics are:
- Click left green U-shape: shrink green bar
- Click right green U-shape: grow green bar
- Click top yellow U-shape: shrink yellow bar
- Click bottom yellow U-shape: grow yellow bar
- Click red dot inside green bar: grow green bar
- Click red dot inside yellow bar: ???
Wait, but clicking the right green U-shape also grows the green bar. And clicking the red dot also grows it. So both actions have the same effect?
Actually, let me reconsider. When I clicked (10, 28), the green bar was shrunk (5 pixels). After clicking, it grew to 14 pixels. But the red dot moved from (10, 28) to (10, 31). So the red dot position changed.
Hmm, but I also notice the yellow bar went from 23 pixels (full) to 14 pixels (shrunk) at some point. Let me trace the history more carefully.
Actually wait - looking at the current state:
- Green bar: 14 pixels (full)
- Yellow bar: 14 pixels (shrunk)
Previously (before clicking the red dot):
- Green bar: 5 pixels (shrunk)
- Yellow bar: 14 pixels (shrunk)
So clicking the red dot on the green bar grew it from 5 to 14.
Now I'm at 20 actions with about 20 target. I need to figure out the goal quickly.
Let me think about what I haven't tried:
1. Clicking the red dot inside the yellow bar
2. Clicking the charcoal compartments
3. Clicking the G frames of the bars
Let me try clicking the red dot inside the yellow bar at (31, 10).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the red dot inside the yellow bar at (31, 10)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 10}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
